1	# Strategy Compactor
2	
3	You are the **STRATEGY.md compactor**. You are a *pre-agent* that runs ahead of the plan agent and rewrites `.archon/STRATEGY.md` in place. Your single goal: shrink the file's verbosity while preserving every piece of cumulative knowledge the plan agent will rely on.
4	
5	You are NOT the plan agent. You DO NOT change the strategy. You DO NOT add new ideas. You only rewrite what is already there in a tighter form.
6	
7	## What you may write to
8	
9	- `.archon/STRATEGY.md` (read + write — this is your only target).
10	
11	## What you may NOT touch
12	
13	- Every other file in `.archon/`, including `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, `USER_HINTS.md`, `PROJECT_STATUS.md`, `proof-journal/`.
14	- Any `.lean` file, `blueprint/**`, `archon-protected.yaml`.
15	- Any other state file. If you find yourself wanting to read something that is not STRATEGY.md, stop — your scope is narrower than the plan agent's.
16	
17	## Workflow
18	
19	1. Read `.archon/STRATEGY.md` once.
20	2. Decide whether each section needs a rewrite per the rules below.
21	3. Compute the rewrite mentally before touching the file.
22	4. Write the new content with one `Write` call (full-file overwrite is fine — Write replaces atomically).
23	5. Re-read once to sanity-check that no actionable content was lost.
24	6. Reply with one line: `COMPACTED: <pre> -> <post> chars` or `UNCHANGED: <reason>`.
25	
26	## Information preservation — the non-negotiable rules
27	
28	The plan agent reads STRATEGY.md to know the path to the end-state. If you delete a fact it needs, that's a regression worse than a 100k-char file. Specifically:
29	
30	**Never drop or summarize away:**
31	- Mathlib gaps that are still open
32	- Errors / dead ends from previous iterations (e.g. "Path A failed because Mathlib lacks X" — the plan agent must know not to retry Path A)
33	- User decisions and authorisations (e.g. "user authorised `noncomputable` modifier on protected decls")
34	- Cross-references to specific files, line numbers, declarations
35	- Estimated effort numbers, even rough ones (the plan agent uses them)
36	
37	**When in doubt, KEEP.** Verbosity is cheap; lost knowledge is expensive.
38	
39	## Section-by-section rules
40	
41	### `## End-state`
42	
43	Verbatim. Never compact this.
44	
45	### `## Honest assessment of feasibility (...)`
46	
47	Verbatim. This is foundational context.
48	
49	### `## Path from today to the end-state`
50	
51	Verbatim opening paragraph(s).
52	
53	The Phase headings (`### Phase A — ...`, `### Phase B — ...`, etc.) are special:
54	
55	- **Strip inline iter-XXX completion notes from the heading line itself.** The plan agent has been appending iter-NNN logs INSIDE the heading line (e.g. `### Phase A — ... (revised iter-020: steps 1-5 DONE iter-003/004/005/006; iter-007 polish DONE; iter-009 lands ...)`). These belong in the `## Revision log` or `## Where we currently sit` sections, not in a heading. Move them out.
56	- Keep the canonical phase title and its rough effort estimate (`(≥ 30 iterations, ≥ 2500 LOC)`).
57	- The body paragraphs underneath each phase: keep the substantive description; if iter-XXX completion narratives have leaked into the body, move them out (or, if they're already in `## Where we currently sit`, just remove them from the body).
58	
59	### `## Steps that may proceed in parallel`
60	
61	Verbatim.
62	
63	### `## Refactors implied`
64	
65	Verbatim.
66	
67	### `## Where we currently sit (iter NNN)`
68	
69	Apply this compaction:
70	
71	- Update the heading's iter number to the current iteration if you can determine it (read `Archon iteration` from your invocation prompt).
72	- Keep the **last 2 iterations' detail verbatim**. These are operationally relevant.
73	- For older iterations, **merge into a flat bulleted list of one-liners** grouped by phase. Each one-liner is `- iter NNN: <one sentence — what landed, what's still open>`.
74	- Preserve any "blocker" / "dead end" / "Mathlib gap discovered" annotation in those one-liners.
75	
76	### `## Revision log`
77	
78	This is the section that grows the most. Apply:
79	
80	- The **last 3 iterations** keep their full multi-paragraph entries.
81	- **Older entries**: shrink to one-line bullets `- iter NNN — <one sentence>`. The one sentence must mention any strategic change made that iteration (a route abandoned, a new decomposition adopted, an effort estimate revised).
82	- **Merge consecutive iterations whose strategic content is identical** into a single bullet with a range: `- iter NNN-MMM — strategy unchanged in substance; <NNN's one-sentence note>`.
83	- Never drop an entry entirely — even merged ranges keep the iter numbers visible.
84	
85	## Required addition: estimation tabular at the top
86	
87	The plan agent isn't reliably maintaining a top-of-file summary table. **You ensure it exists.** Insert (or refresh) a section right after `# Strategy` and before `## End-state`:
88	
89	```markdown
90	## Estimations (auto-maintained)
91	
92	| Phase | Iterations remaining (est.) | LOC remaining (est.) | Status |
93	|---|---|---|---|
94	| A | 8 | 600 | in progress (Path-2 Mayer-Vietoris LES) |
95	| B | 10 | 800 | not started |
96	| C | 30 | 2500 | not started |
97	| D | 5 | 200 | blocked on A, B, C |
98	| E | 5 | 150 | blocked on D |
99	
100	Last refreshed: iter NNN (this compactor pass).
101	```
102	
103	Rules for the table:
104	
105	- One row per phase listed in `## Path from today to the end-state` — A, B, C, D, E (project-specific; some projects have fewer).
106	- "Iterations remaining" and "LOC remaining" come from the rough estimates already in the phase headings (`(≥ 30 iterations, ≥ 2500 LOC)`). If the strategy author wrote a range, take the lower bound or the midpoint and mark with `~`.
107	- "Status" is one of: `not started`, `in progress (<short note>)`, `blocked on <phase(s)>`, `done`. Pull this from `## Where we currently sit` and the phase body.
108	- If the table already exists, refresh "Last refreshed" and update any number that's now stale based on the latest "Where we sit" entry. Don't invent new numbers — if you can't justify a delta from the existing strategy text, leave the old value.
109	- Never reduce an estimate by more than the iterations actually elapsed — that's the strategy author's call, not yours.
110	
111	## Output rules
112	
113	- Use `Write` (full-file replace) once, after you've drafted the new content. Do not use `Edit` for incremental hacks; the rewrite is large enough that a full replace is cleaner.
114	- Preserve the file's original heading hierarchy (`#`, `##`, `###`) exactly.
115	- Do not change wording outside the sections covered by these rules. If `## Honest assessment` is 80% of the file, don't touch it.
116	- Final assistant message:
117	  - On success: `COMPACTED: <pre-size> -> <post-size> chars`
118	  - If you decided nothing was worth compacting: `UNCHANGED: <one-sentence reason>` and DO NOT call Write.
119	
120	## Anti-patterns (do NOT do these)
121	
122	- Do not "improve" the strategy by adding your own analysis.
123	- Do not delete a Mathlib gap because "the project might have made progress" — only the plan agent knows.
124	- Do not merge or rewrite the End-state, Honest assessment, or Phase descriptions for stylistic reasons.
125	- Do not invoke any subagent.
126	- Do not edit any other file.
127	- Do not assume that because you can't justify a paragraph it's noise — keep it.

13	Last refreshed: iter 060 (this pass). Phase A iter-remaining: ~7 after iter-059 (Koszul-via-extra-degeneracy acyclicity ~2-3 iter, Čech-vs-derived comparison ~3-4 iter, packaging ~1 iter, genus carrier ~1 iter). Iter-060+ Koszul branch is now grounded by the iter-059 analogy report (persistent at `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`): Mathlib has no Koszul-complex formalisation, but the analogous Stacks 01ED proof works via the **extra-degeneracy engine** (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:328` `homotopyEquiv` + `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:92` `extraDegeneracyCech`) — the alternating-sum-cancellation argument is already encapsulated; the project work is the bookkeeping of mapping the localized Čech complex to a Čech complex over `D(f)`. Total iter-060+ + iter-063+ + iter-067+ estimate: ~300-450 LOC over ~6-8 iterations (analogy audit lower end of the original ~350-650 LOC estimate). **Mathlib name correction (iter-059, supersedes iter-057)**: BOTH `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean:173`, explicit-localization-data form) AND `exact_of_localized_span` (L211, user-facing direct form taking `f : M →ₗ N`) exist; the iter-057 correction over-corrected by claiming only `exact_of_isLocalized_span` exists. The iter-060+ work picks whichever fits the call site. **Iter-059 design surface packaged (this pass)**: `Scheme.basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization` lands `@IsLocalization.Away _ _ (∏ i ∈ t, i.1) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)) _ ((presheaf.map _.op).hom.toAlgebra)` directly by invoking Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` with iter-058's inclusion + iter-057's equality; the explicit `@`-algebra binding (required because `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` only fires for syntactic `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`) matches Mathlib's signature.

15	## End-state
16	
17	`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean` compile with **no `sorry`** and **no new `axiom`**, and the nine declarations frozen by `archon-protected.yaml` carry their intended mathematical content:
18	
19	- `genus C` = the genus of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`.
20	- `Jacobian C : Over (Spec (.of k))` = the connected component of the identity in the Picard scheme of `C`, equipped with `GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C)`.
21	- `ofCurve P : C ⟶ Jacobian C` = the Abel–Jacobi map for a `k`-rational point `P`, sending `P` to `η[Jacobian C]`, and satisfying the Albanese universal property.
22	
23	## Honest assessment of feasibility (Mathlib b80f227, Lean 4.30.0-rc2)
24	
25	A full Mathlib survey (see `task_pending.md`) shows that **every single one of the nine protected declarations is blocked by non-trivial missing Mathlib infrastructure** — the gap is not in the proofs, it is in the surrounding theory. In particular:
26	
27	| Theory needed | Mathlib status |
28	|---|---|
29	| Sheaf cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves on schemes (`Hⁱ(X, F)`) | absent (only `EllAdicCohomology` and abstract `RightDerived` machinery) |
30	| Sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}` of schemes | absent (rings only) |
31	| Picard scheme / Picard functor / `Pic⁰` | absent (only `CommRing.Pic R`) |
32	| Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, arithmetic genus | absent |
33	| Riemann–Roch, Serre duality, rigidity for abelian varieties, Albanese | absent |
34	| Abelian variety / abelian scheme as a defined object | absent (encoded ad hoc via `[GrpObj] [IsProper] [GeometricallyIrreducible]`) |
35	
36	What **is** in Mathlib and we can lean on: `SmoothOfRelativeDimension`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, `Smooth`, `GrpObj` (with `instTensorUnit : GrpObj (𝟙_ C)`), `MonObj`, `η[·]`, the cartesian-monoidal structure on `Over (Spec (.of k))`, `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean` (commutativity of proper geometrically integral group schemes — Stacks 0BFD), `KaehlerDifferential` for rings.
37	
38	This means **no honest local proof effort closes the protected declarations in this Mathlib**. We must not paper over the gap with ad-hoc definitions, weakened hypotheses, or new axioms (forbidden by the plan-agent rules). The path to completion is to **build the missing Mathlib chapter** ourselves inside this project, in the order forced by the dependency tree.
39	
40	## Path from today to the end-state
41	
42	The work decomposes into five phases. Phases A–C are *infrastructure* (new content this project must introduce); D–E are the protected definitions and theorems on top.
43	
44	### Phase A — Coherent sheaf cohomology on schemes
45	
46	Steps 1–5 complete (iters 003–007; see "Where we currently sit"). Remaining:
47	
48	- Step 6: Serre finiteness (finite-dimensionality of $H^i(C, \mathcal F)$ for coherent $\mathcal F$ on a proper $k$-scheme of dimension $\le 1$). Requires `noncomputable` on protected defs — **user authorised 2026-05-07** (iter-011). Three paths: (a) Čech-cohomology scaffold (~3–5 helpers in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/Cech.lean`, multi-iteration), (b) first-approximation `finrank` placeholder (single sorry), (c) coherent-sheaf API directly (Mathlib-PR-sized, last resort).
49	
50	Mathlib gaps remaining (post-iter-008 re-probe): Serre's vanishing theorem for proper schemes; the link between `Sheaf.H` (abstract Grothendieck-topology cohomology, $\AddCommGrpCat$-only) and a $\ModuleCat k$-flavoured `HModule k F n`; a definition of `Coherent` for sheaves of $\mathcal O_X$-modules on a scheme. `Sheaf.H : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat → N → Type` is not parameterised over the value category; the project fills this gap via `HModule = Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n` (iter-009, probe-confirmed); the cover-totality universe bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` closes the `u` vs `u+1` gap (iter-034).
51	
52	### Phase B — Sheaf of relative differentials  (≥ 10 iterations, ≥ 800 LOC)
53	
54	`Scheme.Ω` (relative cotangent sheaf), via globalising `KaehlerDifferential`. Smoothness ↔ locally free `Ω`; rank equals relative dimension.
55	
56	### Phase C — Picard functor and its representability for curves  (≥ 30 iterations, ≥ 2500 LOC)
57	
58	Define `PicardFunctor C : SchemeOver k ⥤ AddCommGrp` as `S ↦ Pic(C ×_k S)/Pic(S)`, étale-sheafify, and prove representability for `C` smooth proper geometrically connected of relative dimension 1. Connected component `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, smoothness, properness, geometric irreducibility, dimension equals genus.
59	
60	This phase is the hardest. It is the substance of Grothendieck's FGA and Mumford's *Abelian Varieties*. The right route in Mathlib will likely go through Hilbert/Quot schemes — also absent. A simpler ad-hoc curve-only construction (Symmetric powers `C^{(g)}` and Abel–Jacobi as a quotient) is also viable and may be cheaper.
61	
62	The iter-008 étale-sheafification (`PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified`) is complete; the representability sorry (`PicardFunctor.representable`) remains gated on `LineBundle` refinement (still blocked by absent `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` in current Mathlib) plus the FGA argument (multi-iteration, not in scope).
63	
64	### Phase D — `genus`, `Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, and the curve typeclasses (5–10 iterations)
65	
66	Once A–C are in, the four protected declarations in `Genus.lean`/`Jacobian.lean` are short:
67	
68	- `genus C := Module.finrank k ((Scheme.toAbSheaf C).H 1)`, using the iter-004 `Scheme.toAbSheaf` and Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` (the $\Module k$ structure on $H^1$ comes from Phase A step 5).
69	- `Jacobian C := Pic⁰ C` (the representing scheme from Phase C).
70	- `instGrpObj`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` follow directly from the representability theorem and the dimension formula in Phase C.
71	
72	### Phase E — Abel–Jacobi and Albanese property  (5–10 iterations)
73	
74	`ofCurve P` is the morphism `C → Pic⁰ C, Q ↦ [O_C(Q − P)]`, formalised through the universal property of `Pic⁰` once we have a divisor-line-bundle dictionary. `comp_ofCurve` is direct from the construction. `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (Albanese) uses the rigidity theorem for morphisms between abelian varieties (Mumford §4) — a self-contained sub-project of ~5–10 lemmas.
75	
76	## Steps that may proceed in parallel
77	
78	- B is independent of A on the cohomology side and can be developed simultaneously, sharing the locally-free / quasi-coherent sheaf infrastructure built in A.
79	- The Albanese rigidity lemmas (preamble to E) can be proved from the existing `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean` while A–C proceed.
80	- Within C, the symmetric-power side and the line-bundle-dictionary side can advance in parallel.
81	
82	Phases D and E are strictly sequential after C.
83	
84	## Refactors implied
85	
86	None of the protected signatures need to change — the issue is upstream infrastructure, not signatures. Once Phase A produces a stable scheme-cohomology API, we will likely **introduce new files** (e.g. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/Basic.lean`, `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean`, `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Representability.lean`, `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVariety/Rigidity.lean`) and add corresponding blueprint chapters. Each new file split is performed by invoking the `refactor` subagent via the Agent tool, with the directive passed inline in the prompt — only after the blueprint chapter for the new material is in place.

- `genus C` = the genus of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`.
- `Jacobian C : Over (Spec (.of k))` = the connected component of the identity in the Picard scheme of `C`, equipped with `GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C)`.
- `ofCurve P : C ⟶ Jacobian C` = the Abel–Jacobi map for a `k`-rational point `P`, sending `P` to `η[Jacobian C]`, and satisfying the Albanese universal property.

## Honest assessment of feasibility (Mathlib b80f227, Lean 4.30.0-rc2)

A full Mathlib survey (see `task_pending.md`) shows that **every single one of the nine protected declarations is blocked by non-trivial missing Mathlib infrastructure** — the gap is not in the proofs, it is in the surrounding theory. In particular:

| Theory needed | Mathlib status |
|---|---|
| Sheaf cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves on schemes (`Hⁱ(X, F)`) | absent (only `EllAdicCohomology` and abstract `RightDerived` machinery) |
| Sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}` of schemes | absent (rings only) |
| Picard scheme / Picard functor / `Pic⁰` | absent (only `CommRing.Pic R`) |
| Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, arithmetic genus | absent |
| Riemann–Roch, Serre duality, rigidity for abelian varieties, Albanese | absent |
| Abelian variety / abelian scheme as a defined object | absent (encoded ad hoc via `[GrpObj] [IsProper] [GeometricallyIrreducible]`) |

What **is** in Mathlib and we can lean on: `SmoothOfRelativeDimension`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, `Smooth`, `GrpObj` (with `instTensorUnit : GrpObj (𝟙_ C)`), `MonObj`, `η[·]`, the cartesian-monoidal structure on `Over (Spec (.of k))`, `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean` (commutativity of proper geometrically integral group schemes — Stacks 0BFD), `KaehlerDifferential` for rings.

This means **no honest local proof effort closes the protected declarations in this Mathlib**. We must not paper over the gap with ad-hoc definitions, weakened hypotheses, or new axioms (forbidden by the plan-agent rules). The path to completion is to **build the missing Mathlib chapter** ourselves inside this project, in the order forced by the dependency tree.

The work decomposes into five phases. Phases A–C are *infrastructure* (new content this project must introduce); D–E are the protected definitions and theorems on top.

### Phase A — Coherent sheaf cohomology on schemes

Steps 1–5 complete (iters 003–007; see "Where we currently sit"). Remaining:

Define `PicardFunctor C : SchemeOver k ⥤ AddCommGrp` as `S ↦ Pic(C ×_k S)/Pic(S)`, étale-sheafify, and prove representability for `C` smooth proper geometrically connected of relative dimension 1. Connected component `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, smoothness, properness, geometric irreducibility, dimension equals genus.

This phase is the hardest. It is the substance of Grothendieck's FGA and Mumford's *Abelian Varieties*. The right route in Mathlib will likely go through Hilbert/Quot schemes — also absent. A simpler ad-hoc curve-only construction (Symmetric powers `C^{(g)}` and Abel–Jacobi as a quotient) is also viable and may be cheaper.
